info.name: Complete Investigator - Investigator Class
info.description: Investigator class, base features, and the Detective and Exterminator occult specializations.
info.author: Mage Hand Press

description.p: Supernatural detectives and monster slayers, investigators track occult threats, uncover weaknesses, and use ritual magic and guile to defeat monsters and conspiracies.
Hit Dice: 1d8 per investigator level. Hit Points at 1st Level: 8 + your Constitution modifier. Hit Points at Higher Levels: 1d8 (or 5) + your Constitution modifier per investigator level after 1st.

Armor: light armor. Weapons: simple weapons, hand crossbows, heavy crossbows, longswords, rapiers, shortswords. Tools: one gaming set. Saving Throws: Intelligence, Dexterity. Skills: choose three from Arcana, Athletics, Deception, History, Insight, Intimidation, Investigation, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Persuasion, Stealth, Sleight of Hand, and Religion.

Leather armor; a dagger and a spear or any simple weapon; a light crossbow and 20 bolts or a hand crossbow and 20 bolts; a dungeoneer's pack or one kit you are proficient with; a grimoire and a material component pouch.

sheet.description: A supernatural detective and monster slayer.

setters.set: A supernatural detective and monster slayer who relies on expertise, rituals, trinkets, and monster-hunting insight.
Light armor, simple weapons, hand crossbows, heavy crossbows, longswords, rapiers, shortswords, one gaming set, and one investigator skill.

description.p: At 1st level, choose two of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either chosen proficiency. At 6th level, choose two more proficiencies to gain this benefit.

sheet.description: Choose two skill proficiencies; double your proficiency bonus for checks with them.

description.p: At 6th level, choose two more of your proficiencies to gain the benefit of Expertise.

sheet.description: Choose two more proficiencies for Expertise.

description.p: You maintain a grimoire brimming with magical rituals. At 1st level, it contains four 1st-level spells of your choice that have the ritual tag from any class spell list. You can cast spells in your grimoire only as rituals unless you learn them by another means.

Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can add one additional ritual spell from the Investigator Rituals list to your grimoire at no cost. The Ritual Level column of the investigator table gives the maximum spell level you can add.

You can copy ritual spells you find into the grimoire if they are on the Investigator Rituals list and of a level you can add. For each spell level, transcription takes 2 hours and costs 50 gp for rare inks.

Bonus Rituals: the class treats specific spells as if they had the ritual tag. The custom spells in this file include Investigator Ritual support where relevant. For official PHB spells that the class treats as rituals, use this feature text to track the rule if the spell is not listed by the selector.

Spellcasting Ability: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for investigator rituals and spells granted by investigator features. Spell save DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier. Spell attack modifier = your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier.

sheet.description: You maintain a grimoire and cast its spells as rituals. Intelligence is your investigator spellcasting ability.

description.p: You have advantage on Intelligence checks related to researching creatures, spells, and other ancient or secret lore.

sheet.description: Advantage on Intelligence checks to research creatures, spells, and ancient or secret lore.

description.p: Beginning at 2nd level, you can hastily perform any ritual spell in your grimoire that has a casting time of 1 action, completing the ritual as a bonus action. If the ritual requires spell components worth 100 gp or less, you cast it without using spell components.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1), and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. You gain one additional use at 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th level.

sheet.description: Cast a 1-action grimoire ritual as a bonus action; ignore components worth 100 gp or less.

description.p: At 3rd level, choose an occult specialization, an area of expertise in handling eldritch threats. Your specialization grants features at 3rd, 6th, 10th, and 14th level. Investigators of each specialization also collect trinkets; once you use a trinket, you cannot use another until you finish a short or long rest. You can use trinkets one more time at 5th level, 11th level, and 17th level.

sheet.description: Choose an occult specialization. Your trinket uses are 1 per short or long rest, increasing to 2 at 5th, 3 at 11th, and 4 at 17th.

description.p: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you cannot increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

sheet.description: Increase one ability score by 2 or two ability scores by 1, or choose another improvement option if enabled.

description.p: Starting at 5th level, once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, the creature is treated as if it has vulnerability to the weapon damage. Additional weapon damage added by spells and class features such as hunter's mark, Sneak Attack, and Finisher are not doubled. The attack, and all other attacks made against the creature until the start of your next turn, are considered magical for bypassing damage resistance and immunity.

sheet.description: Once on your turn when you hit with a weapon attack, treat the target as vulnerable to the weapon damage; attacks against it count as magical until your next turn.

description.p: By 7th level, you always keep holy symbols and blessed items on your person. When you use this feature, choose one effect. You regain use of this feature when you finish a short or long rest.
Banish: cast banishment without using a spell slot. If the target is a fey, fiend, or undead and fails its saving throw, it also takes radiant damage equal to your investigator level.
Miracle Healing: as an action, touch a willing creature. It regains hit points equal to twice your investigator level. This has no effect on undead or constructs.

Warding: as an action, trace a holy symbol on a creature within 5 feet. For 1 minute, aberrations, celestials, fey, fiends, and undead which touch the target or hit it with a melee attack take 1d12 radiant damage.

sheet.description: Choose Banish, Miracle Healing, or Warding.

description.p: At 9th level, you cannot be possessed or charmed, and you gain resistance to psychic and necrotic damage.

sheet.description: You cannot be possessed or charmed; you have resistance to psychic and necrotic damage.

description.p: Beginning at 11th level, once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 2d8 damage. If the creature has less than half its hit points remaining, you can instead deal an extra 4d8 damage.

sheet.description: On a weapon hit, deal +2d8 damage, or +4d8 if the target is below half hit points.

description.p: At 13th level, you uncover a magical secret. You can cast one of the following spells once without expending a spell slot: mirage arcane, plane shift, reverse gravity, sequester, or teleport. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

sheet.description: Cast mirage arcane, plane shift, reverse gravity, sequester, or teleport without a spell slot.

description.p: At 15th level, you can also cast one of the following spells without expending a spell slot: antimagic field, glibness, maze, or mind blank. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

sheet.description: Cast antimagic field, glibness, maze, or mind blank without a spell slot.

description.p: At 17th level, you can also cast one of the following spells without expending a spell slot: astral projection, gate, or weird. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

sheet.description: Cast astral projection, gate, or weird without a spell slot.

description.p: Beginning at 18th level, you can cast protection from evil and good without using a spell slot or spell components.

sheet.description: Cast protection from evil and good without a spell slot or components.

description.p: By 20th level, select five ritual spells in your grimoire with a casting time of 1 action. You can cast these spells as a bonus action without using a use of Rushed Incantation, and without your grimoire on hand. If the ritual requires spell components worth 100 gp or less, you cast it without using spell components.

sheet.description: Choose five 1-action grimoire rituals; cast them as a bonus action without spending Rushed Incantation.

description.p: Prowling at the edge of darkness, detectives chase down clues and pull on threads to unravel conspiracies which bring darkness into the world.
3rd Level: Investigator's Hunch, Trinkets. 6th Level: Predictive Intuition. 10th Level: Interrogator's Instinct. 14th Level: Power of Deduction.
Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Investigation skill and add double your proficiency bonus to checks you make with it.

sheet.description: You gain Investigation proficiency and double your proficiency bonus on Investigation checks.

description.p: You have collected magical trinkets to aid you in tracking supernatural creatures and unraveling mysteries. Once you use one of your trinkets, you cannot use another one until you finish a short or long rest; uses increase with the investigator Trinkets feature.
Glass Medallion: as a bonus action, cast invisibility, targeting yourself only, without a spell slot or components.
Fogstone Periapt: as a bonus action, cast misty step without a spell slot or components.
Skeleton's Key: as a bonus action, cast knock without a spell slot or components.

sheet.description: Use Glass Medallion, Fogstone Periapt, or Skeleton's Key.

description.p: By 6th level, as a bonus action you examine the movements of a creature you can see within 30 feet. You can add 1d4 to your next attack roll targeting that creature before the start of your next turn, or subtract 1d4 from the creature's next attack roll against you before the start of your next turn.

sheet.description: Choose a creature within 30 ft. Add 1d4 to your next attack against it, or subtract 1d4 from its next attack against you before your next turn.

description.p: At 10th level, you can tell if a creature that you can hear speaking is charmed, possessed, or otherwise enchanted to speak against its will, and you have advantage on any ability check you make to determine if you hear a lie.

sheet.description: You can sense compelled speech and have advantage on checks to determine whether you hear a lie.

description.p: Starting at 14th level, you can use your action to examine a creature you can see within 30 feet, making a chain of logical deductions. For the next minute, you have advantage on Intelligence and Charisma checks you make to interact with that creature, and you have advantage on attack rolls against it. Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

sheet.description: Examine a creature within 30 ft. For 1 minute, gain advantage on Int/Cha checks to interact with it and on attack rolls against it.

description.p: An exterminator suffers no monster to live. Trained in the art of slaying aberrations, fiends, and undead, you stand against evil where others falter, and draw your blade before others recognize a threat.
3rd Level: Bonus Proficiencies, Trinkets. 6th Level: Monster Slayer. 10th Level: Silvered Edge. 14th Level: Killer Instinct.
Starting when you choose this specialization at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons and medium armor.

sheet.description: You gain proficiency with martial weapons and medium armor.

description.p: You have collected magical trinkets to aid you in the elimination of monsters and other fiendish threats. Once you use one of your trinkets, you cannot use another one until you finish a short or long rest; uses increase with the investigator Trinkets feature.
Consecrated Whetstone: as a bonus action, cast magic weapon without a spell slot or components.
Gilded Dragon Scale: as a bonus action, gain resistance to one damage type of your choice for 1 minute.
Wyverntooth Necklace: when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, use your bonus action to activate this trinket, dealing an additional 2d6 acid damage to the creature.

sheet.description: Use Consecrated Whetstone, Gilded Dragon Scale, or Wyverntooth Necklace.

description.p: Beginning at 6th level, when you use the Attack action, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once) and regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

sheet.description: After you take the Attack action, make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

description.p: Starting at 10th level, whenever you deal damage to a creature with a weapon attack, it cannot regain hit points until the start of your next turn. Additionally, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points, it cannot be raised as an undead creature nor be returned to life for 7 days.

sheet.description: Creatures you damage with weapon attacks cannot regain hit points until your next turn. Creatures you reduce to 0 HP cannot be raised as undead or revived for 7 days.

description.p: Starting at 14th level, you can use your Exploit Weakness feature twice on your turn, but cannot use it against the same target more than once.

sheet.description: Use Exploit Weakness twice per turn, but not against the same target more than once.
